qa_pairs.question: When did the Advanced Research Computing infrastructure renewal begin?
qa_pairs.answer: The Advanced Research Computing infrastructure renewal began in winter 2024–2025.

qa_pairs.question: When were new systems from the infrastructure renewal made available to users?
qa_pairs.answer: New systems were made available to users throughout 2025 and early 2026.

qa_pairs.question: What improvements have resulted from the Advanced Research Computing infrastructure renewal?
qa_pairs.answer: The changes have improved High Performance Computing (HPC) and Cloud services for Canadian researchers, offering faster processing speeds, greater storage capacity, and improved reliability.

qa_pairs.question: What percentage of equipment was replaced during the infrastructure renewal?
qa_pairs.answer: Nearly 80% of the equipment that had been approaching end-of-life was replaced.

qa_pairs.question: Which new system replaced Béluga?
qa_pairs.answer: Rorqual replaced Béluga.

qa_pairs.question: What system replaced Cedar?
qa_pairs.answer: Fir replaced Cedar.

qa_pairs.question: Which new system replaced Niagara and Mist?
qa_pairs.answer: Trillium replaced Niagara and Mist.

qa_pairs.question: What new system replaced Graham?
qa_pairs.answer: Nibi replaced Graham.

qa_pairs.question: Will the Cloud interface change due to the Arbutus upgrade?
qa_pairs.answer: No, as a virtual infrastructure, there is no change to the Cloud interface for Arbutus.

qa_pairs.question: Why are outages and reductions unavoidable during the infrastructure transition?
qa_pairs.answer: Outages and reductions are unavoidable due to constraints on space and electrical power during the installation and transition to the new infrastructure.

qa_pairs.question: What is recommended when planning research programs or graduate examinations during the infrastructure transition?
qa_pairs.answer: We recommend that you consider the possibility of outages when you plan research programs, graduate examinations, etc.

qa_pairs.question: What is the status of the Arbutus Cloud upgrade?
qa_pairs.answer: The upgraded Arbutus Cloud is now in production and available to RAC 2026–2027 recipients.

qa_pairs.question: What is the deadline for migrating existing resources from legacy Arbutus for RAC recipients and RAS users?
qa_pairs.answer: Both RAC recipients and RAS users with a 2025–2026 allocation on legacy Arbutus are responsible for migrating existing resources before August 31, 2026.

qa_pairs.question: When can users begin migrating to the new Arbutus Cloud?
qa_pairs.answer: Migration may begin as soon as access to the new Arbutus Cloud is available.

qa_pairs.question: What happens to 2025–2026 RAC allocations on legacy Arbutus during the migration period?
qa_pairs.answer: 2025–2026 RAC allocations will remain active on legacy Arbutus during the migration period.

qa_pairs.question: How can projects with existing Arbutus RAS Cloud allocations request provisioning for the new system?
qa_pairs.answer: Projects with existing Arbutus RAS Cloud allocations must submit a request to cloud@tech.alliancecan.ca to be added to the provisioning queue.

qa_pairs.question: What new authentication methods does the new Arbutus Cloud introduce?
qa_pairs.answer: The new Arbutus Cloud introduces single sign-on (SSO) authentication with multi-factor authentication (MFA).

qa_pairs.question: What must users select when logging into the new Arbutus Cloud?
qa_pairs.answer: Users must select “Authenticate using Digital Research Alliance of Canada” when logging in.

qa_pairs.question: Where can users find documentation for Arbutus migration and multi-factor authentication?
qa_pairs.answer: Documentation is available through the Arbutus Migration Guide and Multifactor Authentication links.

qa_pairs.question: What is the status of the Béluga compute service?
qa_pairs.answer: The Béluga compute service, which was stopped with the deployment of Rorqual, will not return to service, and no restoration or reactivation is planned.

qa_pairs.question: When will Béluga storage quotas be reduced for data deletion or archiving?
qa_pairs.answer: Storage quotas on /project, /home, /scratch, and /nearline for Béluga will be reduced in January 2026 to allow data deletion or archiving only.

qa_pairs.question: When will the temporary storage system (/scratch) on Béluga be decommissioned?
qa_pairs.answer: The temporary storage system (/scratch) on Béluga will be decommissioned on February 28, 2026.

qa_pairs.question: When will access to all remaining data on Béluga end?
qa_pairs.answer: Access to all remaining data on Béluga will end on June 20, 2026.

qa_pairs.question: Is Béluga Cloud impacted by the decommissioning of the Béluga compute cluster?
qa_pairs.answer: No, Béluga Cloud (beluga-cloud) is a separate infrastructure and is not impacted by the decommissioning of the Béluga compute cluster.

qa_pairs.question: When was the Niagara compute cluster retired?
qa_pairs.answer: The Niagara compute cluster was retired on September 30, 2025.

qa_pairs.question: Where can users find migration guidance for transitioning from Niagara?
qa_pairs.answer: For migration guidance from Niagara, users can refer to the 'Transition from Niagara to Trillium' document.

qa_pairs.question: When was the Mist compute cluster retired?
qa_pairs.answer: The Mist compute cluster was retired on September 16, 2025.

qa_pairs.question: To which system should users transition new work from Mist?
qa_pairs.answer: Users should transition new work to Trillium.
